structure.title: Overview
structure.summary: The fragment is a material specification sheet for steel grade 817M40 (EN 24). It outlines the scope of the standard, lists equivalent international designations (e.g., ISO 36CrNiMo4, AISI 4340, DIN 1.6582), and defines the permissible chemical composition ranges for key elements (C, Si, Mn, P, S, Cr, Ni, Mo). It also sets required mechanical properties, specifying a supplied hardness between 180 HBS and 230 HBS, an austenite grain size of 5 – 8 (per IS 2853/ASTM E112), and an inclusion rating according to AS 4163/ASTM E45 (Thin Series).

structure.title: Equivalent Specifications
structure.summary:
The excerpt is a material specification sheet for the alloy steel 817M40 (EN 24). It outlines the supplier‑required standards and includes:

* **Purpose** – Defines the technical supply conditions for 817M40 steel.  
* **Equivalent standards** – Lists comparable designations across major standards (BS 970, ISO 683‑1, AISI/SAE 4340, JIS G4103, DIN 17200, IS 11185).  
* **Chemical composition limits** – Specifies permitted ranges for C, Si, Mn, P, S, Cr, Ni, and Mo.  
* **Hardness** – Sets the acceptable as‑supplied hardness between 180 HBS (minimum) and 230 HBS (maximum).  
* **Austenite grain size** – Requires a grain‑size number of 5 to 8 according to IS 2853/ASTM E112.  
* **Inclusion rating** – References the AS 4163/ASTM E45 “Thin Series” classification (details not shown).  

Overall, the document provides the key technical criteria—chemical make‑up, mechanical properties, micro‑structural limits, and standard equivalents—that a supplier must meet for this steel grade.

structure.title: Chemical Composition Limits
structure.summary: The partial document outlines the specifications and supply conditions for steel grade 817M40 (EN24). It includes an overview of the standard’s purpose, a table of equivalent international designations (e.g., BS 970, ISO 683‑1, AISI 4340, JIS SNCM439, DIN 1.6582, IS 11185), and detailed chemical composition limits for C, Si, Mn, P, S, Cr, Ni, and Mo. It also defines the required hardness range (180–230 HBS), the acceptable austenite grain size (ASTM E112 range 5–8), and references the inclusion rating criteria (AS 4163/ASTM E45 – Thin Series).

structure.title: Hardness as Supplied
structure.summary: The document specifies the supply and technical requirements for steel grade 817M40 (EN24). It outlines the applicable standards and equivalent designations (e.g., BS 970, ISO 683‑1, AISI 4340, JIS SNCM439, DIN 17200, IS 11185). Detailed limits for the chemical composition are provided, covering carbon, silicon, manganese, phosphorus, sulphur, chromium, nickel, and molybdenum. The required hardness range for the material as supplied is 180–230 HBS. Austenite grain size must fall within the ASTM E112 range of 5 to 8, and the inclusion rating follows the thin‑series criteria of AS 4163/ASTM E45.

structure.title: Austenite Grain Size (IS 2853/ASTM E112)
structure.summary: The document is a material specification for the alloy steel 817M40 (EN 24), outlining the supplier‑required technical conditions. It begins with an overview of the standard, then lists equivalent designations across international standards (BS, ISO, AISI/SAE, JIS, DIN, IS). The core of the specification details the acceptable chemical composition ranges for C, Si, Mn, P, S, Cr, Ni, and Mo, followed by the required hardness limits (180–230 HBS) for the supplied material. Additional quality criteria include the permissible austenite grain size (ASTM E112 range 5–8) and an inclusion rating according to AS 4163/ASTM E45 (Thin Series).

structure.title: Inclusion Rating (AS 4163/ASTM E45) - Thin Series
structure.summary: The document defines the supplier‑required technical specifications for steel grade 817M40 (EN 24). It lists equivalent international standards (e.g., ISO 683‑1 36CrNiMo4, AISI 4340, JIS SNCM439, DIN 1.6582, IS 11185) and details the permissible chemical composition ranges for C, Si, Mn, P, S, Cr, Ni and Mo. It sets hardness limits (180–230 HBS), austenite grain‑size requirements (ASTM E112, size 5‑8), and inclusion‑rating limits for both thin and thick series per AS 4163/ASTM E45. The required microstructure is a uniform ferrite‑pearlite matrix with banding not exceeding 0.050 mm, and the material must be free of internal/external defects such as cracks, seams, porosity, slag, inclusions, dendritic structures, or segregation. Decarburization depth must comply with IS 11185 tables. Heat‑treatment is limited to annealed/normalized conditions as‑rolled or forged, with a black surface finish. Each heat number must be accompanied by a test certificate covering chemistry, hardness, grain size, and inclusion rating. Finally, bars are to be color‑coded (green and red ends) for identification.

structure.title: Inclusion Rating (AS 4163/ASTM E45) - Thick Series
structure.summary: The excerpt specifies detailed quality and documentation requirements for steel bars. It defines inclusion ratings (max 3.0, with a “Thick Series” limit of 2.0) for four inclusion types—sulfide, alumina, silicate, and globular—per AS 4163/ASTM E45. It mandates a uniform ferrite‑pearlite microstructure free of harmful bands larger than 0.050 mm (per IS E 407). Bars must be free of internal and external defects such as cracks, seams, laps, porosity, slag, inclusions, dendritic structures, and segregation (per IS 11371). Decarburization depth must not exceed limits in IS 11185 tables 6‑8. The material shall be supplied in the annealed or normalized condition resulting from hot rolling or forging, with a black surface finish as‑rolled/forged. Each heat must be accompanied by a test certificate covering chemical composition, hardness, grain size, and inclusion rating. Finally, bars are to be color‑coded with green and red end caps for identification.

structure.title: Micro Structure (IS ASTM E407)
structure.summary:
The excerpt is a technical specification for steel bars that outlines the quality and inspection requirements a supplier must meet. The main points covered are:

1. **Inclusion Ratings** – Limits for non‑metallic inclusions are defined for four inclusion types (Sulfide, Alumina, Silicate, Globular) with maximum ratings of 3.0 for the “Standard Series” and 2.0 for the “Thick Series” per AS 4163/ASTM E45.

2. **Micro‑structure Requirements** – The steel must exhibit a uniformly distributed ferrite‑pearlite matrix free of harmful banding; any banding must be less than 0.050 mm.

3. **Freedom from Defects** – The material must be free of internal and external surface defects such as cracks, seams, laps, excessive porosity, slag, inclusions, dendritic structures, and segregation (per IS 11371).

4. **Decarburization Limits** – The depth of surface decarburization must stay within the limits specified in IS 11185 (tables 6‑8).

5. **Heat‑Treatment Condition** – Bars are to be supplied in the annealed or normalized condition as hot‑rolled or forged.

6. **Surface Condition** – The as‑delivered surface shall be black, typical of rolled or forged steel.

7. **Test Certificate** – Suppliers must furnish a test certificate for each heat number, covering chemical composition, hardness, grain size, and inclusion rating.

8. **Identification Marking** – Bars must be color‑coded with green and red ends for easy material identification.

Overall, the document sets out precise inclusion limits, micro‑structural and defect criteria, permissible decarburization depth, required heat‑treatment and surface state, documentation, and labeling guidelines for the steel bars.

structure.title: Freedom from Defects (IS:11371)
structure.summary:
The excerpt is a technical specification checklist for steel bars (likely carbon‑steel grades). It outlines the quality and inspection requirements that each heat must meet before shipment, including:

* **Inclusion ratings** – limits for non‑metallic inclusions (sulfide, alumina, silicate, globular) according to AS 4163/ASTM E45, with maximum ratings of 3.0 (general) and 2.0 for the “Thick Series.”
* **Micro‑structure** – the steel must exhibit a uniformly distributed ferrite‑pearlite matrix with banding no greater than 0.050 mm; any doubt is resolved by measurement.
* **Freedom from defects** – the material must be free of internal and external surface flaws such as cracks, seams, laps, harmful porosity, slag, inclusions, dendritic structures, or segregation (per IS 11371).
* **Decarburization depth** – the surface decarburization shall not exceed the limits given in IS 11185 tables 6‑8 (as referenced in IS 6396).
* **Heat‑treatment condition** – the bars shall be delivered in the annealed or normalized condition as‑rolled or forged.
* **Surface condition** – the as‑rolled/forged surface shall be black.
* **Documentation** – a test certificate must accompany each heat, confirming chemical composition, hardness, grain size, and inclusion rating.
* **Identification** – bars are to be color‑coded (green and red at the ends) for material identification.

Overall, the document defines the permissible inclusion levels, required micro‑structural quality, defect‑free criteria, allowed decarburization, heat‑treatment and surface states, certification, and visual identification for the supplied steel bars.

structure.title: Depth of Decarburization (IS:6396)
structure.summary:
The excerpt outlines the quality‑verification requirements for steel bars, organized into a series of technical checks and specifications:

1. **Inclusion Rating (AS 4163/ASTM E45)** – Provides maximum allowable inclusion limits for four inclusion types (Sulfide, Alumina, Silicate, Globular). Two rating tables are shown: a “Thick Series” with a 3.0 max rating, and a more restrictive rating with a 2.0 max limit.

2. **Micro‑Structure (IS ASTM E407)** – Requires a uniformly distributed ferrite‑pearlite matrix free of harmful banding; any band exceeding 0.050 mm must be measured and documented.

3. **Freedom from Defects (IS 11371)** – Bars must be free of internal/external surface defects, including cracks, seams, laps, porosity, slag, inclusions, dendritic structures, and segregation.

4. **Depth of Decarburization (IS 6396)** – The permissible depth of surface decarburization is limited to the values given in IS 11185 (Tables 6‑8).

5. **Heat‑Treatment Condition** – Bars are to be supplied either annealed or normalized, as delivered by hot rolling or forging.

6. **Surface Condition** – The as‑received surface should be black, typical of rolled or forged steel.

7. **Test Certificate Requirement** – Suppliers must issue a test certificate for each heat number, covering chemical composition, hardness, grain size, and inclusion rating.

8. **Miscellaneous** – Bars must be color‑coded (green and red ends) for easy material identification.

Overall, the document fragment specifies the inspection criteria, allowable limits, and documentation needed to ensure that the steel bars meet standard quality and performance standards.

structure.title: Heat Treatment Condition as Supplied
structure.summary:
The excerpt outlines technical specifications and acceptance criteria for steel bars, organized into numbered items:

1. **Inclusion Rating** – Provides maximum allowable inclusion ratings (up to 3.0 for general classification and up to 2.0 for “Thick Series”) for four inclusion types (Sulfide, Alumina, Silicate, Globular) per AS 4163/ASTM E45.

2. **Microstructure (IS ASTM E407)** – Requires a uniformly distributed ferrite‑pearlite structure with no harmful banding; band width must not exceed 0.050 mm, and any ambiguous measurements are to be taken as final.

3. **Freedom from Defects (IS 11371)** – Bars must be free of internal/external surface defects, including cracks, seams, laps, porosity, slag, inclusions, dendritic structures, and segregation.

4. **Depth of Decarburization (IS 6396)** – The decarburized layer must stay within the limits set in Tables 6‑8 of IS 11185.

5. **Heat Treatment Condition** – Bars are to be supplied either annealed or normalized, matching the condition of the hot‑rolled or forged stock.

6. **Surface Condition** – Bars shall be delivered in the “black” (as‑rolled/forged) condition.

7. **Test Certificate** – Suppliers must furnish a test certificate for each heat number covering chemical composition, hardness, grain size, and inclusion rating.

8. **Miscellaneous** – Bars must be color‑coded with green and red end tags for material identification.

Overall, the document defines the quality, microstructural, and documentation requirements for the specified steel bars, referencing Indian Standards (IS) and international inclusion standards.

structure.title: Surface Condition as Supplied
structure.summary:
The excerpt outlines the technical and quality specifications for a steel product, covering the following key areas:

1. **Inclusion Rating** – Specifies maximum permissible inclusion grades (Types A‑D: Sulfide, Alumina, Silicate, Globular) with a rating cap of 3.0 for general assessment and 2.0 for the “Thick Series” per AS 4163/ASTM E45.

2. **Micro‑structure Requirements** – Mandates a uniformly distributed ferrite‑pearlite matrix free of harmful banding, limiting any band width to 0.050 mm (IS E 407).

3. **Freedom from Defects** – Requires the material to be free of internal and external surface flaws, including cracks, seams, laps, porosity, slag, inclusions, dendritic structures, and segregation (IS 11371).

4. **Depth of Decarburization** – Limits decarburization depth according to the values in Tables 6‑8 of IS 11185 (IS 6396).

5. **Heat‑Treatment Condition** – States that the material shall be supplied either annealed or normalized, as hot‑rolled or forged.

6. **Surface Condition** – Requires a black finish consistent with the as‑rolled/forged condition.

7. **Test Certificate** – Obligates the supplier to issue a test certificate for each heat number, covering chemical composition, hardness, grain size, and inclusion rating.

8. **Miscellaneous** – Calls for color‑coding of bars with green and red ends for easy material identification.

structure.title: Test Certificate
structure.summary: The excerpt outlines quality and inspection requirements for steel bars, specifying inclusion ratings for different material types (Sulfide, Alumina, Silicate, Globular) with maximum limits, and detailing micro‑structure expectations such as uniformly distributed ferrite‑pearlite with banding limited to 0.050 mm. It mandates that the material be free of internal and external defects—including cracks, seams, laps, porosity, slag, inclusions, dendritic structures, and segregation—and that decarburization depth not exceed values given in referenced IS standards. The bars must be supplied annealed or normalized in the hot‑rolled or forged condition, with a black surface finish. Suppliers are required to provide a test certificate covering chemical composition, hardness, grain size, and inclusion rating for each heat number. Finally, the document calls for color‑coding the bar ends (green and red) for material identification.

structure.title: Miscellaneous
structure.summary: The excerpt is a technical specification for steel bars that outlines the required quality and inspection criteria. It defines inclusion ratings for different inclusion types (sulfide, alumina, silicate, globular) with maximum limits, and sets a “Thick Series” inclusion rating limit. It specifies micro‑structure requirements, mandating a uniformly distributed ferrite‑pearlite matrix free of harmful banding (≤ 0.050 mm). The document demands freedom from internal and external defects such as cracks, seams, laps, porosity, slag, inclusions, dendritic structures, and segregation. Decarburization depth must stay within the limits given in the referenced IS standards. Heat‑treatment condition must be annealed or normalized as rolled or forged, and the surface condition must be “black” (as rolled/forged). Suppliers are required to provide a test certificate for each heat number covering chemical composition, hardness, grain size, and inclusion rating. Finally, the bars must be color‑coded (green and red ends) for material identification.
